# Zsofia Sztupinszki, M.D., Ph.D.

> Research Fellow at Harvard Medical School and Boston Children's Hospital,
> specializing in cancer genomics, DNA repair deficiencies, and precision medicine.

Zsofia Sztupinszki is a physician-scientist and computational biologist at Harvard
Medical School / Boston Children's Hospital (Computational Health Informatics Program)
and the Danish Cancer Institute. She has authored 47 peer-reviewed publications with
1,561 citations and an h-index of 22 (Google Scholar, 2025). Her primary research focus
is identifying homologous recombination deficiency (HRD) and nucleotide excision repair
(NER) deficiencies through bioinformatics methods applied to cancer genomics data. She
developed the scarHRD R package, an open-source tool for estimating HRD from tumor
sequencing data, which has been cited in over 160 publications. She also created TCGA
Explorer (cancergeneanalytics.com), a no-code pan-cancer analysis platform covering all
30 TCGA cancer types for gene expression, survival, immune infiltration, mutational
signatures, GSEA, and ssGSEA pathway enrichment analyses. She holds patent
WO2021195390A2 (Method for treating cancer).

## Key Research Areas
- Homologous Recombination Deficiency (HRD) detection and biomarkers
- DNA repair deficiency signatures in prostate, ovarian, and bladder cancer
- Cancer immunogenomics and checkpoint inhibitor response prediction
- Machine learning applied to cancer genomics and precision oncology
- Translational cancer research and companion diagnostics

## Tools
- [TCGA Explorer](https://cancergeneanalytics.com/): Free, no-code browser-based platform for pan-cancer gene analysis across all 30 TCGA cancer types. Analyses include gene expression (pan-cancer + normal vs tumor), survival (Cox regression, Kaplan-Meier, optimal cutoff), immune infiltration (TIMER2.0), mutational signatures (COSMIC v2/v3/Indel), differential expression (DESeq2 + GSEA), and ssGSEA pathway enrichment.
- [scarHRD](https://github.com/sztup/scarHRD): Open-source R package for estimating homologous recombination deficiency from tumor sequencing data. Implements three HRD metrics: loss of heterozygosity (HRD-LOH), large-scale state transitions (LST), and telomeric allelic imbalance (TAI).

## Selected Publications
- Oz Kilim et al. Histopathology and proteomics are synergistic for high-grade serous ovarian cancer platinum response prediction. NPJ Precision Oncology, 2025. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41698-025-00808-w
- ALK peptide vaccination restores the immunogenicity of ALK-rearranged non-small cell lung cancer. Nature Cancer, 2023. https://www.nature.com/articles/s43018-023-00591-2
- Comparative Assessment of Diagnostic Homologous Recombination Deficiency associated mutational signatures in ovarian cancer. Clinical Cancer Research, 2021. https://doi.org/10.1158/1078-0432.CCR-21-0981
- Detection of Molecular Signatures of Homologous Recombination Deficiency in Prostate Cancer with or without BRCA1/2 Mutations. Clinical Cancer Research, 2020. https://aacrjournals.org/clincancerres/article/26/11/2673/284540/Detection-of-Molecular-Signatures-of-Homologous
- Cross-reactivity between tumor MHC class I-restricted antigens and an enterococcal bacteriophage. Science, 2020. https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/science.aax0701
